applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused Tanh+HardSwish+Residual kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Fused elementwise operations (Tanh, HardSwish, ResidualAdd) into a single Triton kernel using vectorized block-based loading with BLOCK_SIZE=1024. Uses manual tanh implementation since tl.libdevice.tanh is unavailable on ROCm.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton fused GroupNorm kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: GroupNorm requires computing mean/var over (channels_per_group * H * W) elements per (batch, group). Loop-based approaches caused massive code unrolling or correctness issues.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile wrapper
attempted_but_failed.reason: torch.compile with mode='default' caused Triton GPU compilation errors with 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' legalization failures on ROCm
attempted_but_failed.error: MLIR pass pipeline failure: failed to legalize operation 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local'

attempted_but_failed.name: Loop-based Triton kernel with break
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton does not support 'break' statements in kernels
attempted_but_failed.error: UnsupportedLanguageConstruct: unsupported AST node type: Break

top_bottlenecks: Elementwise ops (Tanh, HardSwish) - now fused in Triton

notes: Achieved SCORE: 61.7 with Triton kernel fusion
Key insight: Use vectorized block-based Triton kernel with tl.arange for parallel loading, not element-by-element loops
BLOCK_SIZE=1024 works well for this problem size (128*64*126*126 = 130M elements)
Manual tanh implementation required: (exp(2x)-1)/(exp(2x)+1) with clamping to [-10, 10]
GroupNorm and Conv2d remain in PyTorch as they are already highly optimized
